shiftedXiSpecialization.omegaRangeUsedHere: real omega; no omega estimate is needed for this identity

halfLineHardyIntegralStatus.label: half-line Hardy integral
halfLineHardyIntegralStatus.reason: For Im z, Im w > 0, exp(i(z-conj(w))r) decays like exp(-(Im z+Im w)r).  Direct integration gives int_0^infty exp(i(z-conj(w))r)dr=1/(i(conj(w)-z)).

plusBranchGramStatus.label: plus branch Gram identity
plusBranchGramStatus.reason: Substituting h_z^+(r)=(2*pi)^(-1/2)E(z)exp(i z r) into the L2(0,infty) inner product gives E(z)conj(E(w))/(2*pi*i*(conj(w)-z)).

minusBranchGramStatus.label: minus branch Gram identity
minusBranchGramStatus.reason: The same calculation with E# gives E#(z)conj(E#(w))/(2*pi*i*(conj(w)-z)).

canonicalHardyImageStatus.label: canonical Hardy/de Branges kernel identity
canonicalHardyImageStatus.reason: Subtracting the minus branch Gram from the plus branch Gram is exactly the de Branges kernel formula.

proof: Because Im(z-conj(w))=Im z+Im w>0, the exponential exp(i(z-conj(w))r) is integrable on the positive half-line.
Evaluating the antiderivative gives the Hardy denominator 1/(i(conj(w)-z)).
The plus branch inner product equals the E(z)conj(E(w)) term of K_E; the minus branch inner product equals the E#(z)conj(E#(w)) term.
Their signed difference is precisely K_E(w,z).
scope: This theorem proves the canonical Hardy branch image only.  The separate augmented closed-cone theorem supplies the KLM pullback and positive-cone closure layers.
